text: The Breast Solid Tumor Rules are effective for cases diagnosed 1/1/2018 and forward. Use these rules to determine the number of primaries and the histology for tumors of the breast (C500-C509).
notes: Fixture: general principle (empty item_ids) with a unit-level dx_date_min overriding the manual-level publication date.

text: The histology coding rules are in hierarchical order. Use the first rule that applies to the case and stop; do not continue through the remaining rules.

Code the histology when only one histologic type is identified. When both invasive duct and invasive lobular carcinoma are present in a single tumor, code 8522/3 (invasive duct and lobular carcinoma).
Table 3. Specific histologies, NOS/NST, and subtypes/variants for breast (excerpt).

codes.8500/2: Ductal carcinoma in situ (DCIS); intraductal carcinoma, noninfiltrating
codes.8500/3: Invasive carcinoma of no special type (carcinoma NST); infiltrating duct carcinoma

notes: Fixture: the one required code_table unit; codes are a subset of the item 522 set in documents/naaccr_data_dictionary_v25.json.

text: Encapsulated papillary carcinoma without documented invasion is coded to behavior /2 (in situ). Code behavior /3 only when invasion is documented in the pathology report.
notes: Fixture: histology-conditioned applicability (only matches when histology 8504 is known; unknown histology widens).
